mission.title: Install and Configure Virtual Kubelet on Kubernetes
mission.description: Virtual Kubelet is an open source Kubernetes kubelet implementation that allows Kubernetes to connect to other APIs, enabling the use of serverless container platforms like ACI and AWS Fargate. Installing Virtual Kubelet allows you to extend your Kubernetes cluster capabilities by integrating with various cloud services.

steps.title: Create a Namespace for Virtual Kubelet
steps.description:
First, create a dedicated namespace for Virtual Kubelet to operate in. Run the following command:
```bash
kubectl create namespace virtual-kubelet
```

steps.title: Deploy Virtual Kubelet
steps.description:
Next, apply the Virtual Kubelet deployment manifest. You can use the following command to deploy version 1.8.0:
```bash
kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/virtual-kubelet/virtual-kubelet/v1.8.0/deploy/virtual-kubelet.yaml -n virtual-kubelet
```

steps.title: Verify the Installation
steps.description:
To ensure that Virtual Kubelet is running correctly, check the status of the pods in the virtual-kubelet namespace:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n virtual-kubelet
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Virtual Kubelet will show the pod running in the `virtual-kubelet` namespace when you execute `kubectl get pods -n virtual-kubelet`. Additionally, resource limits and RBAC settings should be properly configured for optimal operation.

uninstall.title: Remove Virtual Kubelet
uninstall.description:
To uninstall Virtual Kubelet, delete the deployment and the namespace:
```bash
kubectl delete -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/virtual-kubelet/virtual-kubelet/v1.8.0/deploy/virtual-kubelet.yaml -n virtual-kubelet
kubectl delete namespace virtual-kubelet
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade Virtual Kubelet
upgrade.description:
To upgrade Virtual Kubelet, first delete the existing deployment and then apply the new version. For example, to upgrade to version 1.9.0:
```bash
kubectl delete -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/virtual-kubelet/virtual-kubelet/v1.8.0/deploy/virtual-kubelet.yaml -n virtual-kubelet
kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/virtual-kubelet/virtual-kubelet/v1.9.0/deploy/virtual-kubelet.yaml -n virtual-kubelet
```

troubleshooting.title: Pod Not Starting
troubleshooting.description:
Check the logs of the Virtual Kubelet pod for any errors:
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> -n virtual-kubelet
```

troubleshooting.title: Namespace Issues
troubleshooting.description:
Ensure that the virtual-kubelet namespace exists and that the deployment is applied to the correct namespace:
```bash
kubectl get namespaces
kubectl get pods -n virtual-kubelet
```

troubleshooting.title: Resource Quotas
troubleshooting.description:
If you encounter issues with resource allocation, check if there are resource quotas set in the namespace that may be limiting the Virtual Kubelet:
```bash
kubectl get resourcequota -n virtual-kubelet
```

prerequisites.description: Ensure that you have a running Kubernetes cluster and kubectl installed and configured to interact with your cluster.
